title: Lateral Movement + Log Wipe
briefing: Host $TARGET recorded successful network logons (Event ID 4624, logon type 3) for $USER1 coming from internal host $SRC1, and then the security event log was cleared (Event ID 1102). Authenticated movement between internal hosts followed by log clearing is an attacker moving laterally and covering their tracks. Reconstruct the sequence.
dashboard_hint: Discover -> rule.id: 100134 (4624 type 3 network logon) and rule.id: 100133 (1102 log cleared). Correlate targetUserName = $USER1, ipAddress = $SRC1.

questions.explain: Successful type-3 logons from an internal host + log clearing = movement plus track-covering.

questions.prompt: Which internal host did the logons come from?
questions.explain: The 4624 events carry ipAddress = $SRC1 — the pivot host.

questions.prompt: Which account was used to move?
Which event signals the attacker covering tracks?
questions.explain: Event ID 1102 = the security log was cleared, a classic defense-evasion / anti-forensics step.
T1021 for the network logons, T1070.001 for the 1102 log wipe.

options: Isolate both the source and destination hosts
Pull logs from a central collector (not just the wiped host)
Trust the local event log as complete
answer: Isolate both the source and destination hosts
Pull logs from a central collector (not just the wiped host)
questions.explain: The local log is now untrustworthy — pivot to forwarded/SIEM copies, isolate both ends, and rotate the abused account.
